mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 1-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 218.5, signal central bank tightening pressure that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising CPI signals.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates weak supply-side conditions that reduce corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict SPX negative returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month expansion in GDP (GDPC1) signals robust economic growth that drives corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month surge in retail sales (RSAFS) above 367,500 million dollars indicates strong consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a supply-demand imbalance that depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Interaction between INDPRO and UNRATE fails to predict SPX negative returns (p > 0.05) in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 0.25% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS.
